title: selliguea archboldii
intro: Selliguea archboldii, also known as the "Malay water fern," is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate various health issues such as fever, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and supporting wound healing. Selliguea archboldii can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are typically made by steeping the leaves or rhizomes in hot water or a solvent.
